shortDescription.text: Confirms that observed behaviors are consistent with legitimate agentic AI system functionality.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies and confirms behaviors that, while potentially raising initial flags (e.g., file access, internal tool invocation), are verified through external threat intelligence and context to be part of the intended, legitimate functionality of an AI agent or skill. It explicitly states the absence of malicious intent, exploit primitives, or unauthorized actions.

message.text: The 'retry' skill's observed behaviors, including reading and modifying the local session state file (`~/.claude/router-session.json`) and invoking an internal 'Task tool' to spawn a subagent, are consistent with legitimate agentic AI system functionality. External threat intelligence (GitHub issue confirming Claude Code session JSONL files, and ClaudeLog documentation on 'Task tool' mechanics) strongly confirms the intended and benign use of these mechanisms within the Claude Code ecosystem. No confirmed malicious intent (exfiltration, exploitation, C2, persistence), exploit primitives, or malicious infrastructure were identified. This behavior is explicitly verified as non-malicious.
